summary: Independent QA cannot accept commit 0ae7ab4ac983360b31c7592f82066c6320679669 because the locale negotiation does not satisfy the product directive that Chinese system languages resolve to zh-CN. The implementation only recognizes a narrow zh/zh-CN/zh-Hans/zh-Hans-CN/zh-SG set; common Chinese system tags such as zh-TW, zh-HK, and zh-Hans-HK fall back to en-US, and an English variant preference such as en-GB followed by zh-Hans resolves to zh-CN instead of the supported English locale. The rest of the reviewed source scope, handoff evidence, Electron boundary, sim/protocol/save/map authority boundary, Storybook build, Playwright run, and required pnpm validation passed.

Reviewed actual implementation diff. Source changes were limited to allowed Web/UI/Storybook/test paths plus allowed process handoff/state files. No forbidden sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, map-renderer, tools, .github, docs/gates, PROJECT_STATUS, manifest, checksum, or validation-report path changed.
Post-review-commit branch changes were process routing/handoff/task-state files only; no app, package, Storybook, or test source changed after 0ae7ab4a.
No whitespace errors.

M7-I18N-FOUNDATION-001 is REVIEW. Downstream app shell, design tokens, map, inspector, appointment, guidance, regression, content lock, and exit tasks remain DRAFT.
Ready candidates remain M7-DESIGN-TOKENS-ASSET-SLOTS-001 and M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001; no downstream UI implementation was started by this review.

Observed {"enGbThenZh":"zh-CN","zhTw":"en-US","zhHk":"en-US","zhHansHk":"en-US"}; this is the blocking locale canonicalization defect.

Confirmed zh-Hans and persisted legacy zh-Hans canonicalize to zh-CN, unsupported fr-FR falls back to en-US, known route.season.monsoon-risk displays as Monsoon route risk, and the zh-CN app.title code points are 5b63 98ce 8bf8 738b.

acceptance_criteria: Resolve the zh-CN versus zh-Hans locale-code risk explicitly, with systems review, aliases or migration behavior if needed, and tests that prevent drift.
Default to system language on first launch, allow manual language selection, persist player preference, and keep Web and Windows Electron paths behind safe platform adapters.
findings.expected: Any Chinese system language tag must resolve to the supported zh-CN locale per the product directive. English regional tags such as en-GB should resolve to the supported en-US locale and should not be skipped in favor of a later zh-Hans entry.
findings.impact: First launch system-language behavior is wrong for common browser/Electron locale tags. Chinese users with zh-TW/zh-HK/zh-Hans-HK system locales get English instead of zh-CN, while users whose preferred locale is an English regional tag can be switched to Chinese if a later navigator language is zh-Hans. The existing tests do not fail for these drift cases, so downstream UI/localization work would rely on an unstable foundation.
findings.necessary_fix: Canonicalize BCP-47 locale tags by language family for the supported surface: en and en-* resolve to en-US; zh and zh-* resolve to zh-CN unless a future Human Gate adds another Chinese locale. Keep persisted/manual preferences canonicalized to system, en-US, or zh-CN. Add focused unit tests for zh-TW, zh-HK, zh-Hans-HK or equivalent zh-* tags, and en-GB before zh-Hans. Add Web platform or Playwright coverage proving system fallback and persisted migration follow the same canonicalization.

decisions.decision: Request changes because locale negotiation is a foundation-level acceptance criterion and the current implementation fails common Chinese and English regional system locale tags.

The reviewed source diff stays within allowed Web/UI/Storybook/test/process paths and does not alter sim-core, sim-ai, protocol, save-format, map-renderer, desktop IPC/preload, dependencies, tools, CI, docs/gates, or status artifacts.

Do not block this foundation task on remaining prototype UI strings because the accepted M7 task graph assigns complete player-mode shell, map, inspector, appointment, guidance, and regression cleanup to downstream tasks.

risks.summary: If the foundation accepts only a narrow hard-coded alias list, downstream localized UI work can pass tests while real browser or Electron locale tags still resolve to the wrong supported language.
risks.mitigation: Fix canonicalization by language family and add focused tests before rerouting for R2 QA.

risks.summary: Full player-mode string localization and debug/prototype hiding remain incomplete in the current UI.
risks.mitigation: Keep M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001, M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001, M7-DISTRICT-INSPECTOR-QUALITY-001, M7-APPOINTMENT-FLOW-QUALITY-001, M7-PLAYER-GUIDANCE-LITE-001, M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001, and M7-UI-LOCALIZATION-EXIT-001 responsible for final coverage.

non_blocking_notes.summary: This review did not reject the many remaining prototype/player-visible strings in the existing M2-M7 UI because the accepted task graph assigns full player-mode shell, map, inspector, appointment, guidance, and no-prototype/no-debug cleanup to downstream M7 tasks and M7-UI-REGRESSION-MATRIX-001. The current foundation still must fix locale negotiation before those tasks depend on it.

The platform adapter catches localStorage object access but not getItem/setItem exceptions. This was not used as the blocking finding, but adding graceful try/catch coverage while fixing locale tests would better match the safe-adapter intent.

requested_action: Return to the original client_engineer writer to fix locale canonicalization/persistence tests in packages/ui/src/i18n.ts and the related Web/UI/Playwright tests. After repair, rerun the task required validations and route back through systems_architect if the alias policy changes from the accepted systems review; otherwise route directly back to qa_reviewer with an R2 handoff.
